name: bios-build
description: 當使用者輸入 build BIOS、compile BIOS、編譯 BIOS 等相關指令時使用此 skill。清除 Build.log，以非阻塞方式執行 build.bat 並將輸出導向 Build.log，每 3 分鐘檢查編譯是否完成。
system_prompt:
BIOS Build Skill — triggered by: build BIOS, compile BIOS, 編譯 BIOS, or any similar build request.

MANDATORY WORKFLOW (follow every step in order):

Step 1 — Clear the build log:
  run_shell_command: `Remove-Item Build.log -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; New-Item Build.log -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null`
  This empties Build.log so previous output does not interfere with completion detection.

Step 2 — Launch build.bat non-blocking with output redirected:
  run_shell_command: `Start-Process -FilePath 'cmd.exe' -ArgumentList '/c build.bat > Build.log 2>&1 & (if errorlevel 1 (echo BUILD_FAILED >> Build.log) else (echo BUILD_SUCCESS >> Build.log))' -NoNewWindow`
  This starts build.bat in the background. All stdout and stderr go to Build.log. A sentinel line (BUILD_SUCCESS or BUILD_FAILED) is appended when the process exits.
  The command returns immediately — do NOT wait for it to finish here.

Step 3 — Poll every 3 minutes until done:
  Repeat this loop until the build is confirmed finished:
    a. wait(seconds=180)
    b. run_shell_command: `Get-Content Build.log -Tail 20`
    c. Examine the output:
       • Contains `*** BIOS compilation successful!` → build SUCCEEDED. Report success, show the full matching line (which includes the ROM output path), and stop.
       • Contains `BUILD_FAILED` → build FAILED. Show the last 40 lines of Build.log and stop.
       • Neither → still building. Tell the user the build is in progress and loop back to (a).

FINAL RESULT REPORTING:
  • Success: quote the `*** BIOS compilation successful! Output: <path> ***` line so the user knows the ROM location. Also report build duration if "Build end time:" appears in the log (EDK2 format).
  • Failure: show the last 40 lines of Build.log.

RULES:
- Never use run_shell_command to call build.bat directly (it has a 30-second timeout and the build takes much longer).
- Always use Start-Process so build.bat runs as a separate process detached from the command timeout.
- Never give up or report the task as blocked while the build is still running — keep the wait→check loop going.
- If Build.log grows but no completion marker appears after an unusually long time (e.g. > 60 minutes), report the current tail and ask the user whether to keep waiting.
